visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) A young woman with long dark hair, wearing a red and white striped sweater, sits indoors with a man whose face is blurred. She points at him while smiling playfully. (2) Close-up of the woman speaking to the camera; her face is periodically distorted by a bulging-eye and widened-mouth filter. She touches her hair and head. (3) The woman is shown eating a burger with lettuce while a man sits behind her on a sofa. Her facial features are again exaggerated by a digital filter as she makes funny expressions. (4) The woman adjusts her hair and looks thoughtfully at the camera, then laughs heartily with her hands to her face. (5) The woman appears upset or sad, holding her phone. Suddenly, several boxes of Pepero snacks appear on screen, surrounding her in a heart-shaped frame. (6) Close-up shots of various Pepero boxes featuring members of the K-pop group Stray Kids. (7) The woman holds up the Pepero boxes, gesturing and pointing to the text overlays, looking excited and cheerful against a white background.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Subtitle]: 用《換乘戀愛4》語錄測男友反應 💁🏻‍♂️. [Logo]: 🇰🇷. (2) [Subtitle]: 為什麼要裝得這麼顧家？ (原本：왜 이렇게 가정적인 척하는 거야?). (3) [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 麼顧家? 먹고 자?. [Variety]: 韓文：吃飽去睡. [Variety]: 超傻眼. (4) [Variety]: (再試一次). [Subtitle]: 這麼顧家. [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 姑姑的家? 고모집?. (5) [Subtitle]: 謝謝你煮的咖哩很好吃🥰 (feat. 百賢). [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 你在吃漢堡. (그거 햄버거인데). (6) [Variety]: 咖哩口味嗎?. (好現實主義喔.). [Subtitle]: 我的週末是我自己的 (原本：내 주말이 당연히 너 꺼라고 생각하지 마, 내 주말은 내 거야). (7) [Subtitle]: 你不要想得那麼理所當然. [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 你就是我的. (자기 내 꺼니까). [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 你的週末也是我的. (자기 주말도 내 꺼고). (8) [Variety]: ???????? (丟臉). [Subtitle]: 我今天怎麼這麼醜? (原本：나 왜 이렇게 못생겼지?). (9) [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 哎喲~ (에이~). [Subtitle]: 今天是素顏吧? (오늘 쌩얼이지?). [Variety]: (What..?). (10) [Subtitle]: 我有化妝！ [Variety]: (冬瓜加油...). [Variety]: (心情不好). (11) [Subtitle]: 什麼啦?. [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 快到11月11日啊. [Subtitle]: PEPERO DAY?. (12) [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 剛剛只是開玩笑的啦. 不要生氣好嘛. [Subtitle]: 歐巴你知道. (13) [Subtitle]: 包裝是Stray Kids所以買了嗎?. [Subtitle]: 💁🏻‍♂️ - 不.. 不知道. (14) [Subtitle]: 原來這是Stray Kids 聯名包裝. 11/12 PEPERO. [Subtitle]: 除了有創作大賞活動. (15) [Subtitle]: 11/8 - 11/11 在華山進行. [Subtitle]: PEPERO DAY 快閃活動喔. (16) [Slogan]: STAY快到現場和迷孩拍照打卡. [Slogan]: 玩互動小遊戲還能拿好禮喔!. (17) [Subtitle]: 11月11號是在韓國. [Subtitle]: 情侶和朋友們都會特別慶祝的日子. (18) [Subtitle]: 趁這次PEPERO DAY. [Subtitle]: 把平常藏在心裡的感謝偷偷說出來吧.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator/speaker, 1 male partner mostly in background or blurred.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Young East Asian woman with long black hair, wearing a red and white horizontal striped sweater.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: An indoor apartment setting featuring a desk area with a chair and door, shifting to a living room with a neutral-colored sofa and wall decorations.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) High-energy female voice speaks quickly over a bright, rhythmic electronic pop track. (2) A percussive 'pop' sound effect marks a transition to a comedic voice filter section. (3) Male voice responds with a flat, puzzled intonation, followed by a comedic 'whistle' or 'slide' SFX. (4) Frequent hard cuts in the audio track coincide with varied sound effects like 'dings' and cartoonish accents. (5) The music tempo remains steady as the female voice transitions into a clear, enthusiastic promotional delivery style. (6) Audio track concludes with a final energetic phrase and a sharp fade out.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook (Trend Test) -> Comedic Montage -> Brand Pivot -> Event CTA
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To use a trending social media challenge (testing a boyfriend's reaction to famous drama quotes) as an engaging hook to transition into a commercial promotion for Pepero snacks and their upcoming event in Taiwan.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Direct display of the product, naming the brand, and detailing a specific promotional event with dates and locations.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video utilizes popular K-culture trends (Transit Love quotes and K-pop group Stray Kids) to maintain viewer engagement during a sponsored advertisement. The fast-paced editing and filter usage are typical of Douyin/TikTok style short-form content.
